name: European TTF Natural Gas
summary: Day 73-74 (Mon-Tue). **TTF €46.23/MWh MAY 11 (+4.74% 1D, -0.39% 1M, +31.57% YoY, ~+48% from pre-war €31.20 baseline) — STEPPED UP ON TRUMP REJECTION OF IRAN COUNTER-PROPOSAL + ARAMCO 2-5 SHIPS/DAY HORMUZ DETERIORATION.** Markets dossier notes ~€44 baseline May 8 → €46.23 May 11 = +5% on news escalation. **PARTIAL DECOUPLING WITH BRENT NARROWING:** Brent +1.3% 1D May 12 vs TTF +4.74% 1D — TTF playing catch-up to crisis pricing. TTF remains BELOW €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; trigger 'TTF crosses €70/MWh sustained 5 sessions' NOT ACTIVATED. **Henry Hub US Natural Gas +0.7% 1D / +11.6% 1M** — indirect Qatar LNG diversion → European TTF demand → US LNG export pull (markets B-section). Cheniere (LNG) $240.70 (+0.5% 1D, -9.1% 1M) — Q1 2026 $3.5B GAAP loss on derivative MtM (long-term LNG hedged at pre-crisis prices); CCO Anatol Feygin: 'astounded that global gas prices have not risen higher with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz disrupting 100 cargoes a month.' US LNG players SHORT the war via hedge books. **Structural offsets intact:** (a) Hormuz throughput DETERIORATED to 2-5 ships/day per Aramco CEO Nasser (vs ~6/day May 10); (b) European inventory refill window tightening into summer (storage at 5-year seasonal low); (c) Qatar Ras Laffan Trains 4/6 (12.8 mtpa, ~17% Qatar LNG) still 3-5yr recovery. ECB has not characterized Hormuz risk as systemic. **FT framing:** prolonged Hormuz disruption 'could continue to push energy prices higher and reignite inflation pressures' — TTF the most direct read.

current.vs_2022_crisis: still below 2022 crisis levels (€100+); below cascade-intensity threshold €70+

recent_signals.headline: TTF CRASHED -6.8% MAY 6 TO ~€47.4/MWh ON MOU SCOOP — pricing pause as plausibly durable. -28% est 1M / +35% est 3M. MOU 'both sides lift Hormuz blockades' provision priced Qatar Ras Laffan reopening expectation. TTF still below €70+ cascade-intensity threshold; structural offsets (Hormuz closed, inventory tight, Ras Laffan 3-5yr recovery) intact.
